passages.text: Microplastics are tiny pieces of plastic debris, usually less than five millimeters in size, that resulting from the disposal and breakdown of consumer products and industrial waste. Because they do not biodegrade easily, they persist in the environment for centuries. These particles have been found in the deepest parts of the ocean and even in the human bloodstream. Marine animals often mistake them for food, leading to toxic accumulation in the food chain. Governments are now considering stricter regulations on single-use plastics and improved waste management systems to mitigate the impact of this invisible pollutant.

The Silk Road was an ancient network of trade routes that connected the East and West, stretching from China to the Mediterranean. For centuries, it facilitated not only the exchange of goods like silk, spices, and tea but also the spread of ideas, religions, and technologies. This cultural exchange profoundly shaped the civilizations along the route, from the Roman Empire to the Han Dynasty. Despite its name, the Silk Road was not a single path but a series of interconnected land and sea routes. The decline of the Silk Road began with the rise of maritime trade, which offered faster and safer alternatives to the long-distance overland journeys.

passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: No sooner had the company launched its new software than users started reporting critical security flaws.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text: Interviewer: How does your team handle the ethical implications of using AI in recruitment? Manager: ---- Interviewer: That sounds like a solid approach, but how do you verify that the training data itself is unbiased?

questions.question: Despite the rapid advancements in medical technology, a complete cure for certain autoimmune diseases remains ----, leaving researchers to focus primarily on symptom management.

options.A: elusive
options.B: inevitable
options.C: redundant
options.D: lucrative
options.E: obsolete

questions.question: The archaeological team discovered a series of artifacts that ---- the previously held theory about the ancient civilization's trade networks.

options.A: corroborated
options.B: undermined
options.C: perpetuated
options.D: instigated
options.E: alleviated

questions.question: In many developing nations, the transition to renewable energy sources is often hindered by ---- bureaucratic regulations that deter foreign investment.

options.A: streamlined
options.B: cumbersome
options.C: ephemeral
options.D: pragmatic
options.E: arbitrary

questions.question: Although artificial intelligence has significantly improved the efficiency of data analysis, ----.

options.A: it still lacks the intuitive reasoning required for nuanced decision-making
options.B: there is a growing demand for data scientists in the technology sector
options.C: the algorithms have become increasingly complex and difficult to maintain
options.D: many companies have seamlessly integrated these systems into their workflow
options.E: its potential applications in healthcare are virtually limitless

questions.question: ----, the central bank decided to increase interest rates for the third consecutive quarter.

options.A: Unless inflation begins to show signs of stabilizing
options.B: In order to curb the escalating inflation that threatens the economy
options.C: Given that consumer spending has reached an all-time low
options.D: While the government introduced new tax incentives for businesses
options.E: Because the unemployment rate had dropped significantly

questions.question: The documentary not only sheds light on the environmental impact of fast fashion ----.

options.A: but also highlights the unethical labor practices prevalent in the industry
options.B: and it provides a comprehensive overview of global trade agreements
options.C: so consumers are increasingly opting for sustainable clothing brands
options.D: yet it fails to offer viable solutions for everyday shoppers
options.E: because the textile industry is one of the largest polluters worldwide

questions.question: Had the research team secured the necessary funding earlier, ----.

options.A: they would have been able to conduct a much larger clinical trial
options.B: the project will likely yield groundbreaking results in genetics
options.C: they might face fewer obstacles in publishing their findings
options.D: the university has already allocated a budget for next year
options.E: they can hire more assistants to accelerate the data collection

questions.question: The concept of dark matter was (1) ---- proposed to explain the gravitational anomalies observed in galaxy clusters.

options.A: initially
options.B: reluctantly
options.C: inadvertently
options.D: maliciously
options.E: superficially

questions.question: Despite decades of intensive research, dark matter remains undetectable (2) ---- direct observation.

options.A: through
options.B: over
options.C: against
options.D: beneath
options.E: within

questions.question: Scientists believe that it accounts (3) ---- approximately 85 percent of the matter in the universe.

options.A: for
options.B: to
options.C: with
options.D: by
options.E: from

questions.question: If researchers (4) ---- a way to interact with it, our understanding of physics would fundamentally change.

options.A: were to find
options.B: had found
options.C: will find
options.D: are finding
options.E: have found

questions.question: Until then, astrophysicists must rely on indirect evidence, (5) ---- involves studying the cosmic microwave background.

options.A: which
options.B: that
options.C: whose
options.D: where
options.E: whom

questions.question: The ability of certain marine species to adapt to rising ocean temperatures provides crucial insights into the resilience of aquatic ecosystems.

options.A: Bazi deniz turlerinin artan okyanus sicakliklarina uyum saglama yetenegi, su ekosistemlerinin dayanikliligi hakkinda cok onemli bilgiler saglar.
options.B: Artan okyanus sicakliklarina uyum saglayan bazi deniz turleri, su ekosistemlerinin ne kadar dayanikli oldugunu gostermektedir.
options.C: Su ekosistemlerinin dayanikliligi hakkindaki en onemli bilgiler, bazi deniz turlerinin artan okyanus sicakliklarina uyum saglamasindan elde edilir.
options.D: Bazi deniz turlerinin artan okyanus sicakliklarina karsi gosterdigi direnc, su ekosistemlerinin uyum saglama yetenegi hakkinda ipuclari verir.
options.E: Okyanus sicakliklari arttikca bazi deniz turlerinin buna uyum saglamasi, su ekosistemlerinin dayanikliligini anlamamizi saglar.

questions.question: Unless comprehensive measures are taken to reduce carbon emissions, extreme weather events will become more frequent and severe.

options.A: Karbon emisyonlarini azaltmak icin kapsamli onlemler alinmadikca, asiri hava olaylari daha sik ve siddetli hale gelecektir.
options.B: Eger karbon emisyonlarini azaltacak kapsamli onlemler alinmazsa, asiri hava olaylarinin sikligi ve siddeti artabilir.
options.C: Karbon emisyonlarinin azaltilmasi amaciyla alinacak kapsamli onlemler, asiri hava olaylarinin daha sik ve siddetli olmasini engelleyecektir.
options.D: Asiri hava olaylarinin daha sik ve siddetli hale gelmemesi icin karbon emisyonlarini azaltmaya yonelik kapsamli onlemler alinmalidir.
options.E: Kapsamli onlemler alinarak karbon emisyonlari azaltilmazsa, asiri hava olaylarinin yasanma sikligi ve siddeti artmaya devam edecektir.

questions.question: Yapay zeka teknolojilerinin hizli gelisimi, bircok sektorun isleyis bicimini kokten degistirme potansiyeline sahiptir.

options.A: The rapid development of artificial intelligence technologies has the potential to fundamentally change the way many sectors operate.
options.B: Artificial intelligence technologies are developing so rapidly that they can fundamentally change the operations of many sectors.
options.C: The potential to fundamentally change how many sectors operate lies in the rapid development of artificial intelligence technologies.
options.D: Due to the rapid development of artificial intelligence technologies, the way many sectors operate will fundamentally change.
options.E: Many sectors have the potential to change their operations fundamentally because of the rapid development of artificial intelligence technologies.

questions.question: Tarihci, imparatorlugun cokusunun tek bir nedene degil, bir dizi karmasik sosyoekonomik faktore bagli oldugunu iddia etmektedir.

options.A: The historian argues that the collapse of the empire was due not to a single cause, but to a series of complex socioeconomic factors.
options.B: According to the historian, a series of complex socioeconomic factors, rather than a single cause, led to the collapse of the empire.
options.C: The historian claims that it was a series of complex socioeconomic factors, not just a single cause, that brought about the fall of the empire.
options.D: The fall of the empire, as the historian argues, depended on a series of complex socioeconomic factors instead of a single cause.
options.E: The historian maintains that the empire declined not because of a single reason, but due to multiple complex socioeconomic factors.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Immediately after the company released its new software, users began to complain about major security vulnerabilities.
options.B: The company launched its new software because users were complaining about critical security flaws.
options.C: Users started reporting critical security flaws long before the company actually launched its new software.
options.D: If the company had not launched its new software, users would not have reported critical security flaws.
options.E: The company delayed the launch of its new software until users stopped reporting critical security flaws.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: If it hadn't been for the substantial financial support of former students, the university's new research center could not have been constructed.
options.B: The university built the new research facility in order to encourage generous donations from its alumni.
options.C: Despite the generous donations from alumni, the university failed to complete the construction of the new research facility.
options.D: The university would have built the new research facility even if the alumni had not made generous donations.
options.E: Thanks to the new research facility, the university was able to collect generous donations from its alumni.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The government's decision to authorize the building of the disputed dam caused great disappointment among environmental advocates.
options.B: The environmentalists were delighted when the government finally approved the construction of the controversial dam.
options.C: The government decided not to approve the construction of the controversial dam due to the protests of environmentalists.
options.D: Environmentalists persuaded the government to approve the construction of the controversial dam despite public dismay.
options.E: The construction of the controversial dam was delayed because of the dismay expressed by environmentalists and the government.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Everyone agrees that you cannot maintain perfect physical and mental well-being without consuming a well-proportioned diet.
options.B: Some experts believe that a balanced diet is somewhat helpful for maintaining physical and mental health.
options.C: It is often claimed that physical and mental health can be maintained even without a balanced diet.
options.D: A balanced diet is generally considered to be the only factor that influences optimal physical and mental health.
options.E: Most people ignore the fact that a balanced diet is essential for physical and mental health.

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Are you suggesting that I need to make my central claim more obvious right at the beginning?
options.B: Do you think I should add more references to support my conclusion?
options.C: Is it because the topic itself is too complex for a standard essay?
options.D: Should I change the formatting to make the paragraphs look more organized?
options.E: Are you saying that my vocabulary choices are too informal for academic writing?

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: We ensure that all our algorithms are regularly audited for potential biases by an independent third party.
options.B: We primarily focus on maximizing efficiency and reducing the time-to-hire for our clients.
options.C: Ethical implications are not a major concern for us as long as the software functions correctly.
options.D: We let the AI make all the final decisions to completely remove human error from the process.
options.E: Our clients are solely responsible for any ethical issues that arise from using our platform.

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Well, you will have to find a way to cook healthier meals at home if you want to lower those numbers.
options.B: Eating out is fine as long as you order a salad every once in a while.
options.C: There is a new medication that can fix this without you having to change your lifestyle at all.
options.D: You should probably quit your job if it's causing you this much stress and affecting your diet.
options.E: I don't think your diet is the problem; it must be purely genetic.

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: That's terrible news. Did they specify which projects will be affected the most?
options.B: I don't believe that rumor. The dean just promised us more resources last week.
options.C: That's actually great! We can finally focus on our core responsibilities without distractions.
options.D: I suppose I should start looking for a new job in a different university then.
options.E: Did you know that the biology department is getting a massive increase in their budget?

questions.question: Boşluk 25 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: By cultivating crops, humans were able to produce a surplus of food that could be stored for times of scarcity
options.B: Some hunter-gatherer groups fiercely resisted the shift to agriculture, preferring their traditional way of life
options.C: The exact geographical origin of the first domesticated plants remains a subject of intense debate among archaeologists
options.D: Animal domestication occurred roughly around the same time, providing early humans with meat, milk, and labor
options.E: Modern agricultural practices rely heavily on synthetic fertilizers and genetically modified organisms to maximize yield

questions.question: Boşluk 26 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: In today's fast-paced society, however, many individuals sacrifice sleep in favor of work or entertainment
options.B: Some people naturally require less sleep than the recommended eight hours due to a rare genetic mutation
options.C: Dreams occur primarily during the Rapid Eye Movement (REM) stage of the sleep cycle
options.D: Historically, humans practiced biphasic sleep, waking up for a few hours in the middle of the night
options.E: There are various medications available over the counter that can help treat severe cases of insomnia

questions.question: Renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power are crucial for reducing global greenhouse gas emissions. However, they face a significant challenge: intermittency. The sun does not always shine, and the wind does not always blow. ----. This ensures that a stable supply of electricity is available to the grid even when weather conditions are unfavorable for energy generation.

options.A: Therefore, developing efficient and scalable energy storage technologies, like advanced batteries, is absolutely vital
options.B: Fossil fuels, despite their environmental impact, are still the most reliable source of baseload power
options.C: Nuclear power plants generate electricity consistently, but public concern over safety remains high
options.D: Many countries are heavily subsidizing the installation of solar panels on residential properties
options.E: The initial cost of building wind farms offshore is significantly higher than constructing them on land

questions.question: Language is not merely a tool for communication; it also shapes the way we perceive and interact with the world around us. The linguistic relativity hypothesis, often associated with Sapir and Whorf, suggests that the structure of a language influences its speakers' cognition. ----. For instance, languages that assign genders to inanimate objects can affect how speakers describe the qualities of those objects.

options.A: Although the strong version of this theory has been largely discredited, a weaker version is supported by modern psychological research
options.B: Learning a second language in adulthood is significantly more difficult than acquiring it during early childhood
options.C: Many linguists focus entirely on syntax and grammar, completely ignoring the cultural context of language use
options.D: The evolution of language is believed to have coincided with the development of complex tool-making skills in early humans
options.E: Translation software has improved dramatically, making cross-cultural communication easier than ever before

questions.question: In spite of the overwhelming evidence presented by the prosecution, the jury took an unusually long time to reach a verdict.

options.A: Even though the prosecution offered highly convincing proof, the jury's decision-making process was exceptionally protracted.
options.B: Because the prosecution presented so much evidence, the jury was forced to deliberate for a longer time than usual.
options.C: The jury reached a verdict relatively quickly, completely ignoring the overwhelming evidence presented by the prosecution.
options.D: The evidence provided by the prosecution was so confusing that the jury could not reach a verdict for a long time.
options.E: Despite the lack of solid evidence from the prosecution, the jury deliberated for an unusually long period.

questions.question: Had it not been for the timely intervention of the central bank, the country's currency would have collapsed entirely.

options.A: The nation's currency was saved from total ruin solely because the central bank stepped in at the right moment.
options.B: The central bank intervened just in time to prevent a minor drop in the value of the country's currency.
options.C: The country's currency collapsed entirely despite the central bank attempting to intervene in a timely manner.
options.D: If the central bank had intervened earlier, the country's currency would not have faced the risk of collapsing.
options.E: The central bank's timely intervention caused the country's currency to collapse entirely instead of recovering.

questions.question: Virtually all scientists agree that human activities are the primary driver of the current climate crisis.

options.A: There is a near-universal consensus among researchers that the present climate emergency is mainly caused by human actions.
options.B: A small minority of scientists still dispute the idea that human activities are responsible for the current climate crisis.
options.C: Scientists are divided on whether human activities or natural cycles are the main cause of the current climate crisis.
options.D: It is widely believed that human activities are only a minor contributing factor to the current global climate crisis.
options.E: Almost all scientists agree that the current climate crisis will eventually force humans to change their daily activities.

questions.question: (I) The printing press, invented by Johannes Gutenberg in the 15th century, revolutionized the spread of information. (II) Before its invention, books were painstakingly copied by hand, making them rare and expensive. (III) Gutenberg's background as a goldsmith provided him with the skills necessary to create durable metal type. (IV) The mass production of texts led to a significant increase in literacy rates across Europe. (V) Furthermore, it played a crucial role in disseminating the ideas of the Renaissance and the Scientific Revolution.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Coral reefs are among the most diverse and biologically complex ecosystems on Earth. (II) They provide habitat and shelter for roughly one-quarter of all marine species. (III) Marine biologists often spend months at sea collecting data on ocean currents. (IV) In addition to supporting biodiversity, reefs protect coastlines from the damaging effects of wave action and tropical storms. (V) Unfortunately, these vital ecosystems are currently under severe threat from ocean acidification and rising temperatures.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) The concept of emotional intelligence (EI) has gained significant traction in corporate leadership training. (II) It involves the ability to recognize, understand, and manage our own emotions, as well as those of others. (III) Many multinational corporations now use algorithmic screening tools to evaluate resumes. (IV) Leaders with high EI are generally more adept at conflict resolution and fostering a collaborative team environment. (V) Consequently, companies are increasingly incorporating EI assessments into their hiring and promotion processes.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: What is a defining characteristic of microplastics according to the passage?

options.A: They are larger than five millimeters in size.
options.B: They biodegrade very quickly in natural environments.
options.C: They are small plastic fragments that persist for centuries.
options.D: They are only found in consumer products like bottles.
options.E: They have no impact on the human bloodstream.

questions.question: Why are marine animals in danger because of microplastics?

options.A: They are poisoned by the plastic's bright colors.
options.B: They consume them accidentally as if they were food.
options.C: The particles prevent the deep ocean from being cleaned.
options.D: Governments have not yet regulated single-use plastics.
options.E: They are unable to find industrial waste in the ocean.

questions.question: Which step is being considered to reduce microplastic pollution?

options.A: Limiting the production of industrial waste entirely.
options.B: Banning all marine animals from consuming plastic.
options.C: Implementing stricter rules for single-use plastics.
options.D: Disposing of all plastic debris in the deep ocean.
options.E: Reducing the human population's reliance on blood.

questions.question: What was the broader impact of the Silk Road beyond trade?

options.A: It allowed China to dominate the Mediterranean economy.
options.B: It spread religions and technologies across civilizations.
options.C: It encouraged the Roman Empire to produce its own silk.
options.D: It prevented maritime trade from developing for centuries.
options.E: It focused solely on the exchange of spices and tea.

questions.question: How is the Silk Road described physically in the passage?

options.A: A single, highly secured path for silk traders.
options.B: A network of many different land and sea routes.
options.C: A bridge that connected the Han Dynasty to Rome.
options.D: A secret route known only to Chinese merchants.
options.E: A dangerous desert path that was rarely successful.

questions.question: What led to the eventual decrease in the Silk Road's importance?

options.A: The discovery of new land routes in the Mediterranean.
options.B: The collapse of the Han Dynasty and the Roman Empire.
options.C: The development of faster maritime trade options.
options.D: A global reduction in the demand for spices and tea.
options.E: The high cost of maintaining land-based communication.
